steps.title: Parallel lines share the same slope
steps.content:
The given line $y = 42x + 6$ has slope $m = 42$. Since line $s$ is parallel to it, line $s$ also has slope \$42$.

Since line $s$ passes through the origin $(0, 0)$, its equation is:
$$y = 42x$$

steps.title: Substitute the point (4, d) to find d
steps.content:
The point $(4, d)$ lies on line $s$. Substituting $x = 4$ into the equation:
$$d = 42(4) = 168$$
